campaignName: Launch Reel 4 — Two Pitchers, Two Plans
objective: Founder-led Reel proving personalization (two different athletes get two different priorities/drills) instead of repeating the mechanics-freeze-frame device used elsewhere in the launch set.
audience: Parents and coaches who weigh credibility and personalization heavily before buying.
hook: I wanted pitchers to see what coaches are trying to explain.
coreInsight: Generic 'work on your mechanics' feedback isn't actionable; Pitch Nav builds a distinct priority and drill plan from each athlete's own video, which is why two different pitchers get two different plans.
openLoop: The founder names a gap in typical coaching feedback — the viewer watches to see the specific proof that Pitch Nav closes it.
payoff: Two real athletes' priority/drill screens, shown side by side, prove the personalization claim rather than just asserting it.

scenes.cameraDirection: Founder direct-to-camera, medium shot, real footage, speaking the hook line immediately.
scenes.voiceOver: I wanted pitchers to see what coaches are trying to explain.
scenes.soundDesign: Single low tick on the cut in.

scenes.cameraDirection: Founder continues direct-to-camera or cuts to a 3/4-angle B-roll shot while VO continues.
scenes.voiceOver: Every pitcher gets the same generic note — 'work on your mechanics.' That's not specific enough to act on.

scenes.cameraDirection: Real screen recording, split screen of two different athletes' priority cards side by side (different priorities named on each).
scenes.voiceOver: Pitch Nav builds a priority from your video — not a template.

assetReferences.description: Two athletes' priority cards, side by side

scenes.cameraDirection: Screen recording: each athlete's drill list, still split screen, showing two different drill sets.
scenes.voiceOver: Two pitchers. Two different priorities. Two different drill plans.

assetReferences.description: Two athletes' drill lists, side by side

scenes.cameraDirection: Screen recording of the progress calendar view (single screen, not split).
scenes.voiceOver: And a plan that tracks what's actually changing.

scenes.cameraDirection: Cut back to founder direct-to-camera, calm and direct.
scenes.voiceOver: This is what I wanted every pitcher to have.

scenes.cameraDirection: Founder delivers the CTA line directly to camera; wordmark appears beside them, small, bottom-safe.

voiceOverScript: I wanted pitchers to see what coaches are trying to explain. Every pitcher gets the same generic note — 'work on your mechanics.' That's not specific enough to act on. Pitch Nav builds a priority from your video — not a template. Two pitchers. Two different priorities. Two different drill plans. And a plan that tracks what's actually changing. This is what I wanted every pitcher to have. Start your Pitch Nav analysis.
bRollInstructions: Founder must be real, on camera — no AI-generated presenter. Both athletes' screens must be real accounts (anonymized/first-name-only if needed) with genuinely different priorities, not the same data duplicated.
editingNotes: This Reel bookends on the founder (opens and closes on camera) instead of a static end card — a deliberate structural difference from the other four launch Reels. Frame changes every 1-3 seconds. No price shown; this is a trust-building Reel, not a direct-response ad.
soundDesign: Warm, grounded, minimal music bed — founder-trust tone, not tension-building. UI tap accents kept quiet under the split-screen beats so the founder's VO stays the clear focus.

assetReferences.description: Direct-to-camera hook, problem statement, outcome, and CTA
Two athletes' priority cards, drill lists, and one progress calendar
Wordmark beside founder on final beat
